step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("histologic findings in vanishing testes syndrome")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

first, write up to two sentences summarizing your agreement/disagreement with prior conclusions, incorporating newly found evidence. Then give your final answer choice in boxed notation.
I review the previously discussed position—vanishing testes syndrome correlates with non-malignant, progressive atrophy usually involving Sertoli cell-only syndrome, where histology confirms lack of germ cells and normal or mildly reduced testicular size. New evidence reinforces that histologic evaluation definitively identifies the condition via exclusion of germ cells and consistent patterns observed in Sertoli cell-only syndrome, confirming absence of spermatogenic activity. Since no indication exists of active disease progression or concern for malignancy, surgical excision remains unnecessary.
